title: Ranking Keys, Stable Sorts, Ties, and Top-K Reports · Runtime Lens: Topk Boundary
summary: Equal-frequency words appear in hash-dependent order, reverse sorting reverses both count and token unexpectedly, top-k truncation happens before ranking, and repeated runs produce noisy diffs. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the topk boundary increment in deterministic ranked-frequency report. Learners prog…
objectives: Validate k, rank the complete eligible set or use a justified selection algorithm, and specify ties at the cutoff.
Use and test Python stable sorting when earlier order is intentionally retained within equal keys.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-ranking-determinism-2
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-ranking-determinism-2.
# Case 7417: an assistive-technology terminal review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def worked_bookbot_ranking_determinism_2(eligible_words: int = 9, ranked_words: int = 9, top_k: int = 5, ties_resolved: bool = True, repeated_output_equal: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "corpusworked2",
        "policy2dekrow",
        "revision7417",
        "commandworked27417",
        "report2dekrow7417",
        "test7417worked2",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if eligible_words < 0 or ranked_words < 0:
        return False, ranked_words, "rank-shape"
    if ranked_words != eligible_words:
        return False, ranked_words, "rank-coverage"
    if top_k < 1 or top_k > ranked_words:
        return False, top_k, "top-k"
    if not ties_resolved or not repeated_output_equal:
        return False, top_k, "unstable-ranking"
    assert top_k <= ranked_words
    return True, top_k, "deterministic-top-k"

# Evidence: bookbot-topk-boundary-8khgub
# Competency: bookbot-topk-boundary.
# Case 1834: a multilingual community archive intake.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def evidence_bookbot_topk_boundary_8khgub(eligible_words: int = 9, ranked_words: int = 9, top_k: int = 5, ties_resolved: bool = True, repeated_output_equal: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_8khgub = "8khgub-bughk8-1834"
    assert evidence_variant_8khgub
    evidence_axes_8khgub = (
        "corpus8khgub",
        "policybughk8",
        "revision1834",
        "command8khgub1834",
        "reportbughk81834",
        "test18348khgub",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_8khgub)) == 6
    if eligible_words < 0 or ranked_words < 0:
        return False, ranked_words, "rank-shape"
    if ranked_words != eligible_words:
        return False, ranked_words, "rank-coverage"
    if top_k < 1 or top_k > ranked_words:
        return False, top_k, "top-k"
    if not ties_resolved or not repeated_output_equal:
        return False, top_k, "unstable-ranking"
    assert top_k <= ranked_words
    return True, top_k, "deterministic-top-k"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Validate k, rank the complete eligible set or use a justified selection algorithm, and specify ties at the cutoff.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Topk Boundary model · Theory Topk Boundary, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Topk Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Topk Boundary model · Theory Topk Boundary, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 2522 during a large-file migration rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to rank all eligible words with explicit ties and reproducible top-k output; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Validate k, rank the complete eligible set or use a justified selection algorithm, and specify ties at the cutoff.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Topk Boundary model · Theory Topk Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Slicing the first k dictionary entries before sorting returns the top k.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: bookbot-topk-boundary-8khgub.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: bookbot-topk-boundary-8khgub(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_bookbot_topk_boundary_8khgub\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?ranked_words != eligible_words)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?top_k < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not ties_resolved)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not repeated_output_equal)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
